1.1:
The vision of Obadiah.
Thus speaks the Lord GOD concerning Edom:
A summons has gone out from Yahweh;
a herald races among the nations —
'Rise up! Let us march against her!'
1.2:
See what I have done:
I have made you the least of nations —
an object of contempt among them all.
1.3:
Pride has deceived you through and through.
You nest in the mountain crevices;
you make your home in the heights
and say to yourself: 'Who can drag me down?'
But it is pride itself that has blinded you.
1.4:
Soar like the eagle if you can;
set your nest among the very stars —
from there Yahweh will pull you down.
The LORD has declared it.
1.5:
Thieves take only enough and leave the rest.
Harvesters strip a vineyard but leave gleanings behind.
But you, Edom — you have been stripped utterly bare,
nothing left, nothing overlooked.
How completely you have been destroyed!
1.6:
Every cache Esau had has been tracked down;
not a single hidden store escaped the plunderer's eye.
1.7:
Every nation you trusted as an ally
has pushed you to the frontier and abandoned you there.
Your friends deceived you and got the upper hand.
The ones who broke bread with you were the ones who laid the trap.
Not a shred of wisdom remained in Edom — none saw it coming.
1.8:
On that day — Yahweh's own word —
every sage in Edom will be swept away;
no wisdom, no counsel, no discernment
will remain on the highland of Esau.
1.9:
Even your warriors, O Teman, will be struck with terror —
and every man on Esau's mountain
will fall to the slaughter.
1.10:
Your violence against Jacob — your own brother —
has sealed your verdict.
Shame will wrap itself around you like a burial shroud,
and you will be erased from history forever.
1.12:
Three failures on the day your brother fell:
You should not have stood there gloating.
You should not have celebrated Judah's collapse.
You should not have opened your mouth in triumph
on the very day they were in agony.
1.13:
Three more on that same dark day:
You should not have marched through Jerusalem's gate.
You should not have feasted your eyes on their ruin.
You should not have stretched out your hand to plunder.
Each act on the day of their calamity. Each one a betrayal of brotherhood.
1.14:
And the worst of all:
You blocked the mountain passes — hunting down those trying to flee.
You handed the survivors over to their enemies
on the very day they were running for their lives.
1.15:
The Day of the LORD draws near —
and it comes not for Judah alone but for every nation.
The law of return is absolute:
what you did will be done to you;
everything you set in motion will land on your own head.
1.16:
You drank your fill of triumph on my holy mountain.
Now every nation will be made to drink —
not celebration but the cup of judgment —
drinking on and on, swallowing, staggering,
until they vanish from the earth as if they had never been.
1.17:
But Mount Zion will be a place of rescue —
a holy remnant standing on holy ground.
The house of Jacob will repossess
everything that was stripped from them.
1.18:
Jacob will be the fire.
Joseph will be the flame.
Esau will be dry stubble.
Together they will burn through him and leave nothing —
not a single survivor from the house of Esau.
Yahweh has spoken it, and it is done.
1.20:
Even the scattered exiles will return with an inheritance:
those dispersed as far north as Zarephath
will reclaim the land of Canaan;
those exiled to far-off Sepharad — wherever that distant place may be —
will come home to settle the cities of the south.
(Sepharad is the endpoint of diaspora: no exile is beyond the reach of restoration.)
1.21:
Then saviours will ascend Mount Zion
and rule over the highlands of Esau —
and when that day comes,
the kingdom will belong to Yahweh alone.
